items.title: Leaked data shows NT diphtheria cases surged a month before alert was issued

children.content: Internal Northern Territory Health data leaked to the ABC reveals diphtheria case numbers surged in the NT a month before a public health alert was issued. The data also offers the first public insight into the regions hit hardest by the outbreak.

gallery.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

thumbnail.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

items.title: Aboriginal families in Federal Court bid for traditional ownership recognition

children.content: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

image.altText: The coastline of a remote beach.

items.title: This herb could halt a major NT gas project

children.content: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.

image.altText: A small herb plant, green with three leaves growing out of a stone / pebble ground area.

items.title: Average bull price nudges $5,000 at NT's biggest sale

children.content: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.

gallery.altText: Two brahman bulls, white with a hump neck in view, only one of their heads visible, people in the background holding paper.

thumbnail.altText: Two brahman bulls, white with a hump neck in view, only one of their heads visible, people in the background holding paper.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: Alleged murderer's bail in limbo as Tiwi Islands town erupts in riots

children.content: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.

image.altText: A photo from a plane shows a remote community sitting on the edge of an island, part of the aircraft's wing is visible.

items.title: Darwin mum's killer ordered to spend indefinite period in custody

children.content: Sheena Fairfield was stabbed to death outside her family home more than three years ago. Her killer will now spend 20 years in supervised custody.

image.altText: A court sketch of a white man, with auburn curly / wavy long hair, scruffed, looking right, blue shirt.

items.title: 'Happy ending for us': These refugee cricketers fled to Australia for safety

children.content: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

gallery.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.
High-angle shot of huddle of Afghan women cricket players, hot-pink shirts, blue caps, standing on green sports field.
Side-on shot of a row of Afghan women cricket athletes standing in hot-pink and blue uniforms, caps and hijabs among them.

thumbnail.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.
High-angle shot of huddle of Afghan women cricket players, hot-pink shirts, blue caps, standing on green sports field.
Side-on shot of a row of Afghan women cricket athletes standing in hot-pink and blue uniforms, caps and hijabs among them.

items.title: Legal action filed over decision to clear almost 3,000ha of tropical savanna

children.content: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Murray Watt frowns.

items.title: The surprising city claiming to be Australia's street art capital

children.content: Artists say Darwin has surpassed Melbourne as Australia's street art capital
with work underway on a fresh round of murals across the Top End city.

image.altText: A close up painting of a woman's face in purple, pink, blue and black painted on a building viewed through black fence palings

items.title: Australians are watching scrutiny of the Bondi attack through a keyhole

children.content: For now, Australians seeking to understand any intelligence gaps, failures, missed warnings and government failings before the Bondi massacre are expected to accept a keyhole view.

image.altText: Flower Memorial 2025-12-18 10:12:00

avatar.alt: A man with closely-cropped dark hair, a moustache and beard poses for a headshot in a studio, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Vivid Sydney drone shows cancelled after technical failure

children.content: All remaining Vivid Sydney drone shows have been cancelled following a technical failure at Monday night's show, where almost 90 drones fell out of the sky.

image.altText: drones inthe sky during a vivid show in sydney over darlingharbour as some fall from the sky

items.title: Teen's extraordinary recovery after surviving 'most extensive' shark bite

children.content: Fourteen-year-old Sam Nai survived a devastating shark attack with a desperate swim for survival, followed by 20 surgeries to reconstruct his abdomen.

gallery.altText: Sam in hospital bed ready for surgery while his mother and Dr O'Connor smile at him.

thumbnail.altText: Sam in hospital bed ready for surgery while his mother and Dr O'Connor smile at him.

items.title: US judge orders removal of Trump name from Kennedy Center

children.content: US President Donald Trump says his administration will transfer control of the Kennedy Center to Congress, after a judge ordered the removal of his name from the Washington venue and blocked his plans to close it for renovations.

image.altText: The John F. Kennedy Center for Performing Arts is seen in Washington.

items.title: Trump concludes meeting on potential Iran deal without announcing decision

children.content: The US president has been weighing a tentative agreement that would open the Strait of Hormuz and begin talks on Tehran's nuclear program.

image.altText: President Donald Trump speaks during a Cabinet meeting at the White House.

items.title: The 1970s oil shock gave birth to permaculture. This fuel crisis is reviving it

children.content: The tenets of permaculture are attracting a new generation of home gardeners. It's perhaps no surprise, considering the movement has firm roots in the world's last oil crisis in the 1970s.

gallery.altText: A man with a fruit tree it's a vintage shot.

thumbnail.altText: A man with a fruit tree it's a vintage shot.

items.title: 'Ridiculous increases': Cattle market kicks in the rain

children.content: Cattle prices jumped significantly this week as rain fell across key production areas, especially in New South Wales.

image.altText: Cattle next to a rainfall map

items.title: David Szalay was ambushed with an AI sequel to his prize-winning novel

children.content: The Booker Prize winner sat down at the Sydney Writers' Festival to ponder the threat of AI to fiction, share some recommendations and consider whether his book was really about masculinity.

image.altText: David Szalay headshot

items.title: We asked for your modern dating love stories — here is what you told us

children.content: From instant connections to immediate rejections to a sudden change of fate, ABC readers take us on an emotional rollercoaster ride with their modern dating experiences.

gallery.altText: A graphic of two hands clasped

thumbnail.altText: A graphic of two hands clasped

items.title: Transform tinned tuna into a crunchy, golden dinner

children.content: Kishwar Chowdhury says this nostalgic dish is "the ultimate Bangladeshi diaspora invention".

image.altText: A plate of disc-shaped tuna patties on a dinner table.

items.title: The simple, ordinary joy of Kylie Minogue

children.content: In the end, a woman who's had wins and losses, terrors and triumphs, and who regularly goes back to stay with family in suburban Melbourne, has become a music legend of her time, and it's not hard to figure out why.

image.altText: pop star Kylie Minogue sits in a dressing gown on a deck chair smiling pensively.

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Pleas for French Open to move 'unsafe' ad boards after star's nasty fall

children.content: Iga Świątek urges French Open officials to move advertising boards away from the courts after seeing Zeynep Sönmez's tournament end due to injury.

articles.title: De Minaur and Djokovic bundled out of French Open leaving draw wide open

children.content: Seeded stars continue to tumble at Roland-Garros as both Alex de Minaur and Novak Djokovic's campaigns end in the third round.

gallery.altText: Zeynep Sonmez's doubles teammate checks in after fall

items.title: 'Stop the madness': Olympic stadium fight taken to IOC head office

children.content: Opponents of Brisbane's centrepiece Olympic stadium take their concerns directly to the IOC's headquarters in Switzerland as a critical deadline looms.

image.altText: Olympic house in Lausanne

items.title: Australia's marathon queen thought she might never walk again

children.content: As a girl, Kaz Thorburn was told she would never play sport or have children. The mother of three has now run 551 marathons.

image.altText: A woman running along grass near the coast.

items.title: How women over 60 are still being made the butt of the joke in film

children.content: Films are four times more likely to feature a talking animal lead than a woman over 60. These charts explain the state of film for older women.

gallery.altText: Lindsay Lohan and Jamie Lee Curtis peer through a pile of toys in Freakier Friday.
Two women, one wearing dress with embroidery, the other a red pant suit, embrace and pose for cameras and small crowd.

items.title: Matthew Perry was 'on the right path' before inner circle betrayed him

children.content: A trusted assistant, two doctors, a middleman and the "Ketamine Queen": How Matthew Perry's inner circle preyed on the Friends star's addiction before his death.

image.altText: A close up of a smiling Matthew Perry, wearing clear plastic-rimmed glasses, a black suit jacked and a grey v-neck shirt.

items.title: Bondi refuses to answer Trump questions in Epstein probe

children.content: Several survivors of Epstein's abuse gathered outside the Capitol office where the interview was taking place and made their presence known to Pam Bondi as she entered the room.

image.altText: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi arrives for her deposition dressed in a maroon suit

items.title: 'Stand up with us and for us': Artist's passions collide at surf comp

children.content: Surfer Jessi Rebel's journeys with art and identity have beaten similar paths — both inspired by one relative's sage piece of advice.

items.title: Motel owners say Booking.com has a stranglehold on their businesses

children.content: Booking.com is being sued in Europe, and consumer advocates think Aussie travellers might also have a claim.

image.altText: A hand holding a phone with Booking.com on the screen

items.title: Families of police officers killed at Wieambilla feel 'let down' as property sold

children.content: The families of two Queensland police officers shot dead by extremists in 2022 say they feel "deeply let down" after learning through a public real estate listing the property had been sold to a private buyer.

image.altText: A composite image of a man and woman both in police uniforms.

items.title: Karate teacher's 'hair pulls and chokeholds' combat gender-based violence

children.content: Savannah Last has been a "karate kid" since she was two, but when she was attacked by a group of teenagers last year, she felt "hopeless".

gallery.altText: A teenage girl in a white karate outfit, flanked by two older women wearing black dresses.

thumbnail.altText: A teenage girl in a white karate outfit, flanked by two older women wearing black dresses.

items.title: Will Ruth Forrest get an apology for her leaked email?

children.content: The Tasmanian upper house member was livid on Tuesday night after an email she sent to the government was leaked to the media. She wants an apology, but will she get one?

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair looks across the room

items.title: Hopes pinned on promising sequel to prevent a Whyalla wipeout

children.content: "We've seen this movie before" is a phrase that sums up the way many people feel about the Whyalla steelworks' financial predicament but the community is hopeful of a promising new sequel.

image.altText: A structure at Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Should politicians be using AI-generated images?

children.content: A Victorian state MP's use of an apparently AI-generated image raises questions about whether AI has any place in politics.

image.altText: Roma Britnell looks at the camera wearing a pink jacket

items.title: Ski resorts remain optimistic despite forecast for warmer winter

children.content: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.

image.altText: A ski resort field with several skiiers going down a hill

items.title: Why do some people in the Pacific want to make themselves whiter?

children.content: Skin bleaching and lightening products are widely sold in Tonga, which is part of a region with a long history of colourism.
